categories.name: Prompt Injection
categories.description: Malicious inputs manipulate the LLM to override instructions or exfiltrate data via direct or indirect injection.
indicators: unsanitised user input concatenated into prompts
no prompt armoring or delimiters around user-supplied content
agent-to-agent message passing without input validation
system prompt instructions that can be overridden by user content

categories.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure
categories.description: The model reveals PII, credentials, API keys, or confidential system details in its outputs.
indicators: user data or file contents sent to external API without redaction
credentials or secrets embedded in prompts
LLM outputs returned to users without filtering
logging of prompt or response contents containing sensitive data

categories.name: Supply Chain
categories.description: Vulnerabilities in third-party models, datasets, plugins, or packages the agent depends on.
indicators: unpinned or unverified third-party dependencies
use of pre-trained models or embeddings from unverified sources
plugins or tool integrations loaded dynamically without integrity checks
no lockfile or hash verification for dependencies

categories.name: Data and Model Poisoning
categories.description: Corrupted training data or fine-tuning inputs alter model behaviour or introduce backdoors.
indicators: user-controlled input used to update or fine-tune models
RAG or knowledge base documents accepted from untrusted sources without validation
no integrity verification on training datasets or model weights
dynamic prompt templates that can be altered by external data

categories.name: Improper Output Handling
categories.description: LLM outputs passed downstream without sanitisation, enabling XSS, SSRF, code injection, or privilege escalation.
indicators: LLM output rendered as HTML or executed as code without sanitisation
LLM-generated URLs or file paths used in system calls
JSON or structured output from the LLM parsed without schema validation
LLM output written to files without path validation

categories.name: Excessive Agency
categories.description: The agent is granted more permissions, tools, or autonomy than the task strictly requires.
indicators: agent has write, delete, or execute permissions beyond read-only
tool use or function calling with broad scope (e.g. shell execution, database writes)
no human-in-the-loop confirmation for irreversible actions
agent can invoke other agents or external services without approval

categories.name: System Prompt Leakage
categories.description: System prompt instructions or secrets can be extracted by adversarial users.
indicators: system prompt contains API keys, credentials, or sensitive business logic
no guardrails preventing the model from repeating system prompt contents
system prompt accessible or visible in client-side code
error messages that expose prompt fragments

categories.name: Vector and Embedding Weaknesses
categories.description: RAG pipelines or embedding stores susceptible to poisoned documents or retrieval manipulation.
indicators: documents ingested into vector store without content validation
retrieved chunks injected into prompts without sanitisation
no access controls on vector database queries
embedding model loaded from unverified source

categories.name: Misinformation
categories.description: The model generates plausible but false content, leading to incorrect decisions or trust erosion.
indicators: no grounding, citations, or retrieval to back up factual claims
outputs presented as authoritative without disclaimer
no cross-verification or confidence scoring on generated facts
agent makes consequential decisions based solely on LLM output

categories.name: Unbounded Consumption
categories.description: No rate limits or resource controls, enabling DoS, runaway API costs, or resource exhaustion.
indicators: no input size or token limit before calling the API
no rate limiting or per-user quotas on LLM calls
recursive or looping agent patterns without iteration caps
no timeout on API calls or downstream tool invocations
